Simulation Fits

Author

Moses Mburu 

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  fig.width = 11,
  fig.height = 7,
  dpi = 500,
  dev = "png"
)

library(data.table)
library(grid)
library(here)
library(ineqTrees)
library(partykit)

here::i_am("simulation/simulation_fits.qmd")
source(here("R", "report_helpers.R"))
source(here("simulation", "ci_simulation_functions.R"))
simulation_n <- as.integer(params$n)
tuning_folds <- max(2L, as.integer(params$tuning_folds))
baseline_risk <- as.numeric(params$baseline_risk)
poor_share <- as.numeric(params$poor_share)
concentration_strength <- as.numeric(params$concentration_strength)
criterion_types <- c("CI", "CIg", "CIc", "L")

outcome_name <- "health_outcome"
rank_name <- "wealth"
weight_name <- "sample_weight"

# These are the observed covariates available to the tree. The planted
# simulation labels are deliberately left out, so any recovered structure must
# come from the same type of subgroup rules used in the empirical analysis.
predictors <- c("mother_education", "residence", "child_sex", "province")
var_labels <- c(
  mother_education = "Mother's education",
  residence = "Residence",
  child_sex = "Child sex",
  province = "Province"
)
simulation_formula <- as.formula(
  paste(
    sprintf("cbind(%s, %s)", rank_name, outcome_name),
    "~",
    paste(predictors, collapse = " + ")
  )
)
simulation_data <- ci_sim_make_simple_examples(
  n = simulation_n,
  seed = 20260603,
  outcome_name = outcome_name,
  baseline_risk = baseline_risk,
  poor_share = poor_share,
  concentration_strength = concentration_strength
)

# The first dataset is a negative control. The second dataset has a known
# inequality-generating subgroup structure, so the fitted tree can be checked
# against a pattern that was built into the data.
names(simulation_data) <- c(
  "Null simulation",
  "Poor subgroup simulation"
)
tune_simulation_ci_tree <- function(data, seed, force_root_plot = FALSE) {
  # The tuning grid follows the same idea as the main analysis: stopping rules
  # are treated as hyperparameters, and cross-validation chooses the setting
  # that removes the largest share of root-node impurity.
  control_grid <- ci_tree_control_grid(
    minsplit = c(250L),
    minbucket = 50L,
    minprob = 0.01,
    maxdepth = c(7L),
    min_gain = 0,
    min_relative_gain = c(0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2)
  )
  control_grid <- as.data.table(control_grid)
  #control_grid <- control_grid[minbucket <= 0.5 * minsplit]

  # All concentration-index criteria are tuned on the same simulated data. This
  # makes the figures comparable across CI, CIg, CIc, and L.
  tuning <- tune_ci_tree(
    formula = simulation_formula,
    data = data,
    rank_name = rank_name,
    outcome_name = outcome_name,
    weights = data[[weight_name]],
    type = criterion_types,
    control_grid = control_grid,
    v = tuning_folds,
    strata = outcome_name,
    seed = seed,
    metric = c("validation_gain", "relative_validation_gain"),
    refit = FALSE,
    control = control_ci_tune(save_pred = TRUE)
  )

  best <- ci_select_best(
    tuning,
    metric = "relative_validation_gain"
  )

  # The selection table records the validation result for each criterion. This
  # is useful because a split can appear in training but fail to transfer well to
  # held-out folds.
  selection_table <- ci_fit_summary_table(
    tuning,
    selected = best,
    metrics = unique(tuning$summary$metric),
    include_percent = FALSE
  )
  selection_table[
    best,
    terminal_nodes := i.mean_terminal_nodes,
    on = c("type", "grid_id")
  ]
  selection_table[, selection_basis := paste(
    tuning_folds,
    "fold cross-validation"
  )]
  selection_table[, plotted_fit := if (force_root_plot) {
    "Root-only null reference"
  } else {
    "Selected tuned tree"
  }]

  # In the null simulation, small finite-sample gains can create accidental
  # splits. For the plotted negative-control figures we therefore show the root
  # node only, while the tuning table still reports what cross-validation found.
  root_control <- ci_tree_control(
    minsplit = nrow(data) + 1L,
    minbucket = max(1L, floor(nrow(data) / 2L)),
    maxdepth = 0L
  )

  models_by_type <- setNames(
    lapply(seq_len(nrow(best)), function(i) {
      setting <- best[i]

      # Each criterion is refitted with its own selected stopping rule. These
      # fitted trees are the objects passed to ci_report_tree_plot below.
      ci_tree(
        formula = simulation_formula,
        data = data,
        rank_name = rank_name,
        outcome_name = outcome_name,
        weights = data[[weight_name]],
        type = setting$type[1L],
        control = if (force_root_plot) {
          root_control
        } else {
          ci_control_from_row(setting)
        }
      )
    }),
    best$type
  )

  terminal_summaries <- rbindlist(lapply(names(models_by_type), function(type) {
    out <- as.data.table(ci_tree_terminal_summary(models_by_type[[type]]))
    out[, criterion := type]
    data.table::setcolorder(out, "criterion")
    out
  }), fill = TRUE)

  list(
    tuning = tuning,
    best = best,
    selection_table = selection_table,
    models_by_type = models_by_type,
    terminal_summaries = terminal_summaries
  )
}
simulation_fits <- Map(
  tune_simulation_ci_tree,
  data = simulation_data,
  seed = c(20260604L, 20260605L),
  force_root_plot = c(TRUE, FALSE)
)
capture_simulation_tree_plot <- function(scenario_name, type) {
  grid::grid.grabExpr(
    ci_report_tree_plot(
      fit = simulation_fits[[scenario_name]]$models_by_type[[type]],
      data = simulation_data[[scenario_name]],
      outcome_name = outcome_name,
      outcome_label = "U5",
      ci_type = type,
      var_labels = var_labels,
      rank_name = rank_name,
      weight_name = weight_name,
      fontsize = 11
    )
  )
}

simulation_tree_plots <- lapply(names(simulation_fits), function(scenario_name) {
  type_names <- names(simulation_fits[[scenario_name]]$models_by_type)
  setNames(
    lapply(type_names, function(type) {
      capture_simulation_tree_plot(scenario_name, type)
    }),
    type_names
  )
})
names(simulation_tree_plots) <- names(simulation_fits)

simulation_tree_plot_metadata <- list(
  generated_from = "simulation/simulation_fits.qmd",
  criterion_types = criterion_types,
  simulation_parameters = list(
    n = simulation_n,
    tuning_folds = tuning_folds,
    baseline_risk = baseline_risk,
    poor_share = poor_share,
    concentration_strength = concentration_strength
  ),
  selection_tables = lapply(simulation_fits, `[[`, "selection_table"),
  terminal_summaries = lapply(simulation_fits, `[[`, "terminal_summaries")
)

save(
  simulation_tree_plots,
  simulation_tree_plot_metadata,
  file = here("simulation", "simulation_tree_plots.rda")
)

1 Null Simulation

report_compact_table(
  simulation_fits[["Null simulation"]]$selection_table,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Selected tuning settings for the null simulation, by concentration-index criterion"
)
Selected tuning settings for the null simulation, by concentration-index criterion
type grid_id minsplit minbucket minprob maxdepth min_gain min_relative_gain mean_train_gain mean_train_relative_gain mean_validation_gain mean_validation_relative_gain n_train_gain n_train_relative_gain n_validation_gain n_validation_relative_gain std_err_train_gain std_err_train_relative_gain std_err_validation_gain std_err_validation_relative_gain mean_root_objective n_root_objective std_err_root_objective terminal_nodes selection_basis plotted_fit
CI 3 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0 0 0 0 5 5 5 5 0 0 0 0 0.0772 5 0.0223 1 5 fold cross-validation Root-only null reference
CIc 11 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0 0 0 0 5 5 5 5 0 0 0 0 0.0216 5 0.0063 1 5 fold cross-validation Root-only null reference
CIg 7 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0 0 0 0 5 5 5 5 0 0 0 0 0.0054 5 0.0016 1 5 fold cross-validation Root-only null reference
L 16 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.2 0 0 0 0 5 5 5 5 0 0 0 0 0.0027 5 0.0007 1 5 fold cross-validation Root-only null reference 
for (type in criterion_types) {
  if (!type %in% names(simulation_fits[["Null simulation"]]$models_by_type)) {
    next
  }

  cat("\n\n### Null Simulation: ", type, " Criterion\n\n", sep = "")
  ci_report_tree_plot(
    fit = simulation_fits[["Null simulation"]]$models_by_type[[type]],
    data = simulation_data[["Null simulation"]],
    outcome_name = outcome_name,
    outcome_label = "U5",
    ci_type = type,
    var_labels = var_labels,
    rank_name = rank_name,
    weight_name = weight_name,
    fontsize = 11
  )
}

2 Poor-Subgroup Concentration Simulation

report_compact_table(
  simulation_fits[["Poor subgroup simulation"]]$selection_table,
  digits = 4,
  caption = "Selected tuning settings for the poor-subgroup concentration simulation, by concentration-index criterion"
)
Selected tuning settings for the poor-subgroup concentration simulation, by concentration-index criterion
type grid_id minsplit minbucket minprob maxdepth min_gain min_relative_gain mean_train_gain mean_train_relative_gain mean_validation_gain mean_validation_relative_gain n_train_gain n_train_relative_gain n_validation_gain n_validation_relative_gain std_err_train_gain std_err_train_relative_gain std_err_validation_gain std_err_validation_relative_gain mean_root_objective n_root_objective std_err_root_objective terminal_nodes selection_basis plotted_fit
CI 3 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0.2550 0.6120 0.2369 0.5693 5 5 5 5 0.0047 0.0114 0.0229 0.0544 0.4169 5 0.0113 5 5 fold cross-validation Selected tuned tree
CIc 11 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0.3142 0.8092 0.2838 0.7290 5 5 5 5 0.0056 0.0117 0.0193 0.0404 0.3885 5 0.0104 5 5 fold cross-validation Selected tuned tree
CIg 7 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0.0786 0.8092 0.0710 0.7290 5 5 5 5 0.0014 0.0117 0.0048 0.0404 0.0971 5 0.0026 5 5 fold cross-validation Selected tuned tree
L 15 250 50 0.01 7 0 0.1 0.0776 0.9244 0.0732 0.8713 5 5 5 5 0.0012 0.0104 0.0033 0.0260 0.0840 5 0.0022 5 5 fold cross-validation Selected tuned tree 
for (type in criterion_types) {
  if (!type %in% names(simulation_fits[["Poor subgroup simulation"]]$models_by_type)) {
    next
  }

  cat("\n\n### Poor-Subgroup Concentration Simulation: ", type, " Criterion\n\n", sep = "")
  ci_report_tree_plot(
    fit = simulation_fits[["Poor subgroup simulation"]]$models_by_type[[type]],
    data = simulation_data[["Poor subgroup simulation"]],
    outcome_name = outcome_name,
    outcome_label = "U5",
    ci_type = type,
    var_labels = var_labels,
    rank_name = rank_name,
    weight_name = weight_name,
    fontsize = 11
  )
}
